note: The capability layer as a flat list. Each entry maps the playbook's INTENTS to a real tool URL the agent opens via the browser (Chrome MCP), and declares the FIELDS that tool provides — so the engine knows what each capability can return. effect = read-only | write. Replace the urls with your real tools.

capabilities.label: ServiceNow — incident & CMDB
capabilities.what: The incident record, affected CIs, dependency relationships, and similar past incidents.

capabilities.label: Datadog — metrics & telemetry
capabilities.what: Error-rate, latency (p50/p99), saturation, and DB query metrics for the affected service.

capabilities.label: Splunk — logs
capabilities.what: Error logs / stack traces and recurring error patterns around the symptom window.

capabilities.label: Grafana / Tempo — traces
capabilities.what: Distributed traces — where the latency is spent and the call dependency chain.

capabilities.label: GitHub — deploys & change history
capabilities.what: Recent deploys / commits — the 'what changed' and the exact diff.

capabilities.label: Synthetic monitor — replay
capabilities.what: Replay the user journey to confirm recovery from the user's side.

capabilities.label: Runbook — failover / rollback  (WRITE)
capabilities.what: Trigger DB failover, rollback a deploy, restart, or scale. GATED — needs human approval.

capabilities.label: PagerDuty — on-call & escalate  (WRITE)
capabilities.what: Who's on call, and page / escalate. The escalate action is GATED.
